name: go-regression
description: A Go-only diff (main.go) activates the go profile and does NOT activate the k8s profile, even when the source contains K8s-evocative prose like 'deploy' or 'cluster' in comments.
prompt: I've staged the following changes. Run `/kk:review-code` against them.
trap: Model spuriously activates the `k8s` profile because the file contains the word 'deploy' or 'cluster' in a comment, instead of applying the authoritative rule (k8s content signal requires `apiVersion:` + `kind:` at zero indent in YAML, or a Helm/Kustomize filename signal).

assertions.text: Resolver — Active profiles: `main.go` row has `profile=go` with `triggered_by` citing the `.go` extension (per the go profile's DETECTION.md — extension match is authoritative).

Resolver — Active profiles: no row attributes `main.go` (or any file) to `profile=k8s`. The k8s profile does not fire: no YAML document has `apiVersion:` + `kind:` at zero indent, no `Chart.yaml`, no `kustomization.yaml`, no file under a `templates/` directory with a `Chart.yaml` sibling.
Resolver — Loaded checklists: contains `(go, ...)` entries drawn from the Go profile's `review-code/index.md`. No `(k8s, ...)` entry appears in Loaded.

Resolver — NOT loaded: does NOT list any `(k8s, ...)` entry either — the k8s profile did not activate, so its `review-code/index.md` is not consulted and its conditional checklists are neither listed in Loaded nor in NOT loaded.

Reviewer — if findings are emitted, they use the P0/P1/P2/P3 severity structure with `**[file:line]** Brief title`. (The fixture is small and may legitimately produce zero findings; an empty/low-finding review is acceptable here. The eval is primarily about routing, not content.)
